evidence.value: multicenter, open-label, parallel-group, randomized, explanatory, adaptive trial
evidence.quote: This was a multicenter, open-label, parallel-group, randomized, explanatory, adaptive trial
Three temporal COVID-19units in Mexico City, Jalisco, and Tabasco recruited patients
144 were randomized to thermotherapy (n=72) or standard care (n=72)

evidence.value: hospitalized adult patients with mild-to-moderate COVID-19
evidence.quote: Eligible participants were patients with symptoms of COVID-19 (fever, headache, cough, sore throat, myalgias, arthralgias, shortness of breath, anosmia, fatigue, diarrhea, vomiting, or conjunctivitis) who were admitted to hospital upon a compatible clinical presentation, meeting criteria for mild or moderate COVID-19

evidence.value: centralized computer-generated sequence of minimization with a random component of 20%
evidence.quote: Patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio through a centralized computer-generated sequence of minimization with a random component of 20%. The automated assignment system was built with the open-code OxMaR software

evidence.value: open-label; participants and medical staff were not blinded; data analyst was blinded
evidence.quote: Participants and medical staff could not be blinded to the intervention since a medical device was employed. The data analyst (AKG) was blinded to the treatment groups.
Clinical Trial registration: www.clinicaltrials.gov, identifier: NCT04363541.

evidence.value: composite outcome of progression to severe COVID-19, critical COVID-19, or death
evidence.quote: The primary outcome was a composite outcome of progression to severe COVID-19, critical COVID-19, or death
thermotherapy n = 54
144 were randomized to thermotherapy (n=72)

evidence.value: local thermotherapy via an electric heat pad (30 × 40cm) in the thorax continuously for 90min, twice daily (every 12h), for 5days
evidence.quote: The intervention consisted of local thermotherapy via an electric heat pad (30 × 40cm) in the thorax continuously for 90min, twice daily (every 12h), for 5days.
control n = 51
144 were randomized to ... standard care (n=72)

Both the control and intervention groups received standard in-hospital care according to national guidelines. Standard of care consisted of usual in-hospital care, including but not limited to oxygen therapy, thromboprophylaxis, systemic corticosteroids upon supplementary oxygen requirement
The primary outcome of disease progression occurred in 31.4% (16/51) of patients in the control group vs. 25.9% (14/54) of those receiving thermotherapy
seven (12.9%) in the thermotherapy group had at least one AE
The primary outcome of disease progression occurred in 31.4% (16/51) of patients in the control group
Seven (13.7%) patients in the control group ... had at least one AE
risk difference = 5.5%; 95%CI: −11.8–22.7
